perturbation.original_text: Pinetree agrees that all Proprietary Material disclosed shall remain the property of STI, and nothing herein shall be construed as the grant of a license or any other right, either directly or indirectly, by implication, estoppel or otherwise, to any Proprietary Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree agrees that all Proprietary Material disclosed shall remain the property of STI, except that Pinetree will be granted a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the Proprietary Material for internal evaluation purposes during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that nothing should be construed as granting a license to Proprietary Material, but the changed text grants a non-exclusive license, which creates an in-text contradiction and legal uncertainty. The altered clause in Section 4(c) directly contradicts the initial statement about no licenses being granted. This impacts any section relying on the premise of STI retaining exclusive rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pinetree agrees that all Proprietary Material disclosed shall remain the property of STI, except that Pinetree will be granted a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the Proprietary Material for internal evaluation purposes during the term of this agreement. STI has no obligation to disclose any Proprietary Information. STI makes no representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Proprietary Material, and shall have no liability to Pinetree or its Representatives resulting from Pinetree's use of the Proprietary Material, except as may be expressly set forth in any subsequent, definitive written agreement between the parties. This Agreement does not create an agency, partnership, joint venture or other like relationship.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that nothing within the agreement should be construed as granting a license to the Proprietary Material. However, the changed text directly contradicts this by granting Pinetree a non-exclusive, royalty-free license. This creates a direct conflict, as the contradicted text assumes that STI retains exclusive property rights, which is undermined by the granted license. As defined above, this is an inconsistency that leads to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: STI may take any necessary action to compel specific performance of this Agreement or to enjoin any violation of this Agreement so that Proprietary Material is not used in any manner to harm or adversely affect STI.
perturbation.changed_text: STI may only take action to compel specific performance of this Agreement if Pinetree uses the Proprietary Material in a manner that demonstrably and directly causes financial harm to STI.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows STI to seek specific performance or an injunction for any violation, while the altered text limits STI's recourse to cases where Pinetree's use demonstrably and directly causes financial harm. This change in Section 6 creates an in-text contradiction by restricting STI's ability to enforce the agreement, and will influence legal actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Maryland, without regarding to its choice of law provisions. The parties further agree and consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Maryland; thus, in the event that either party initiates an action pursuant to or otherwise governed by this Agreement, the exclusive jurisdiction of such action shall be in Maryland. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may enforce any judgment rendered by such court in any court of competent jurisdiction. STI may only take action to compel specific performance of this Agreement if Pinetree uses the Proprietary Material in a manner that demonstrably and directly causes financial harm to STI.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that STI may take any action to compel performance or enjoin any violation. The changed text limits STI to only taking action if Pinetree's use of Proprietary Material demonstrably and directly causes financial harm. The contradicted text contains the changed text and explicitly limits STI's ability to compel specific performance. Since the changed text restricts the scenarios in which STI can compel specific performance, while the original text grants them broad abilities to do so, there is a clear contradiction created within the document as the changed text limits the scope of the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties and supersedes all other agreements, negotiations and proposals, written and oral, between the parties concerning the above-described Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties with respect to non-disclosure, but does not supersede any prior or contemporaneous agreements related to the Lawsuit or the Settlement Documents.
perturbation.explanation: The original integration clause states that the agreement supersedes all prior agreements. The modified text creates an in-text contradiction because it now carves out exceptions for agreements related to the Lawsuit and Settlement Documents. This contradiction impacts the interpretation of the agreement as a whole, particularly regarding obligations under the Lawsuit and Settlement Documents, it can be found in Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties with respect to non-disclosure, but does not supersede any prior or contemporaneous agreements related to the Lawsuit or the Settlement Documents. This Agreement may only be modified by a written document signed by authorized officers of both parties. This Agreement may be executed in two (2) or more counterparts, by manual or facsimile signature, each of which shall be an original and all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same agreement. SOLOMON TECHNOLOGIES, INC. PINETREE (BARBADOS) INC.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement supersedes all prior agreements. The changed text explicitly states that it does *not* supersede prior agreements related to the Lawsuit and Settlement Documents. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, this still causes an in-text contradiction since the original "entire agreement" clause contradicts the new clause about the Lawsuit and Settlement Documents. This meets the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as different sections (the original and the changed text) provide conflicting agreement terms.

perturbation.original_text: No rights or duties of Pinetree under this Agreement may be assigned without the prior written consent of STI. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Agreement 3 <PAGE> shall be deemed null and void AB INITIO. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective Representatives, successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree may assign its rights and duties under this Agreement to any of its affiliates without the prior written consent of STI. Any other attempted assignment in violation of this Agreement shall be deemed null and void AB INITIO. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective Representatives, successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits assignment without STI's consent. The modified text in Section 8 now allows assignment to Pinetree's affiliates without STI's consent, creating a contradiction regarding assignment rights. This impacts any clause relying on the original restriction, introduces a conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No rights or duties of Pinetree under this Agreement may be assigned without the prior written consent of STI. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Agreement 3 <PAGE> shall be deemed null and void AB INITIO. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective Representatives, successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both state that Pinetree needs STI's consent for assigning rights. However, the changed text allows Pinetree to assign rights to its affiliates without STI's consent. This creates a direct conflict and thus a contradiction between the changed text and the contradicted text, which requires STI's consent for all assignments.

perturbation.original_text: Pinetree must protect the Proprietary Information from unauthorized use or disclosure by exercising the same degree of care that Pinetree uses with respect to information of its own of a similar, highly valuable nature, and Pinetree must, at minimum, use at least reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree must protect the Proprietary Information from unauthorized use or disclosure by exercising reasonable care, but is not required to use the same degree of care that Pinetree uses with respect to information of its own of a similar, highly valuable nature.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 3(b) requires Pinetree to use the same degree of care as with its own highly valuable information, but at minimum, reasonable care. The changed text only requires reasonable care, removing the higher standard. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the required standard of care, impacting any section depending on the original degree of protection. It creates uncertainty about the protective measures Pinetree must implement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pinetree must protect the Proprietary Information from unauthorized use or disclosure by exercising reasonable care, but is not required to use the same degree of care that Pinetree uses with respect to information of its own of a similar, highly valuable nature. c. DUPLICATION. Pinetree shall not copy, duplicate, replicate, decompile or reverse engineer in any manner whatsoever (whether physically, electronically, in writing or otherwise), in whole or in part, any part of the Proprietary Information without the prior written consent of STI, which consent shall be in STI's sole and absolute discretion, and will immediately notify STI in writing of any unauthorized disclosure of the Proprietary Information. At any time upon STI's request, Pinetree shall promptly redeliver to STI, or destroy at STI's direction, all written material in any media or format whatsoever containing, reflecting or in any way derived from the Proprietary Information (including notes, summaries, copies, extracts or other reproductions, in whole or in part), regardless of whether the material was prepared by STI or on STI's behalf. The redelivery or destruction of the Proprietary Information shall be certified by Pinetree in writing to STI, and shall not in any way relieve Pinetree of its obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The original text required Pinetree to exercise the same degree of care as with its own highly valuable information, but at minimum, reasonable care. The changed text only requires reasonable care, removing the higher standard. The Contradicted Text is the same as the changed text, however, the explanation states that this creates a contradiction because the original text required a higher standard of care. The changed text alters the level of care required for Proprietary Information, creating a direct conflict with the initial obligation.
